title: 10 Herbal Teas to Help with Dry Skin
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry skin are a natural remedy that helps to soothe, calm and hydrate dry and irritated skin. They work by providing essential nutrients, antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties to combat dryness and promote skin health. The best herbal teas for dry skin include Calendula officinalis, Urtica dioica, Aloe barbadensis, Symphytum officinale, Aloe vera, Camellia sinensis, Hypericum perforatum, Rosmarinus officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Equisetum arvense. If left untreated, dry skin can lead to discomfort, itching, and cracked skin, significantly impacting daily life, causing embarrassment, and limiting one's ability to engage in physical activities, affecting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is made from the flowers of the marigold plant. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties. When used as a tea, these properties help to soothe and calm dry skin. The antioxidants in calendula tea work to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can dry out the skin. The anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and irritation, making the skin feel smoother and more comfortable. Calendula tea also has a moisturizing effect on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and keep the skin hydrated. This is especially helpful for people with dry skin conditions such as eczema or dermatitis. Drinking calendula tea can also help to promote healing and reduce the risk of infection in wounds and skin irritations.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry skin because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect dry skin from irritation and damage.
Tannins are good for dry skin because they help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from environmental damage, reducing dryness and irritation.
Saponins are good for dry skin because they have moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for dry skin
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal teas that help with dry skin, you will need some green tea leaves, which are actually the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant. First, boil fresh water in a kettle or on the stove. Then, measure out one teaspoon of dried green tea leaves or one tablespoon of fresh leaves for every 8 ounces of water. Once the water is boiling, remove it from the heat and pour it over the green tea leaves in a teapot or a cup. Allow the leaves to steep for 1-3 minutes, depending on your desired tea strength. You can then strain the tea leaves and drink it as is, or add some honey or lemon to taste. Drinking green tea regularly can help to hydrate the skin and reduce dryness, thanks to its high antioxidant content and ability to stimulate blood flow. Additionally, green tea also contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is good for dry skin because it has special powers that help to soften and moisturize the skin. The tea contains a type of plant extract called aloin, which is known for its soothing and healing properties. When we drink aloe barbadensis tea, the aloin gets into our body and helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the skin. This makes our skin feel cooler and less itchy, and helps to lock in moisture so that it doesn't dry out. Aloe barbadensis tea also helps to repair damaged skin cells and improve skin elasticity, making it look smoother and more supple. Drinking aloe barbadensis tea regularly can help to keep our skin hydrated and healthy, especially during dry and cold weather.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for dry skin because they have antioxidant properties that help to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation, resulting in softer and healthier-looking skin.
Flavonoids are good for dry skin because they help to protect the skin from damage, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow to the skin, which can help to lock in moisture and soothe dryness.
Aloe-emodin is good for dry skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm dry, itchy skin, while also promoting skin hydration and reducing skin irritation.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for dry skin
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal teas that can help with dry skin, start by gathering high-quality tea leaves from the Camellia sinensis plant. Camellia sinensis is the plant used to make traditional green tea and black tea, and it has been used for centuries to soothe and moisturize dry skin. Begin by boiling fresh, filtered water in a kettle or on the stovetop. Then, add one teaspoon of loose-leaf Camellia sinensis tea to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Let the tea leaves steep in the boiling water for 3-5 minutes, or until the liquid reaches your desired strength. After the steeping time, remove the tea leaves and let the tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature. Once the tea has cooled, you can drink it as a beverage or use it as a compress to help soothe and moisturize dry skin. To use the tea as a compress, simply soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it to the affected area for 10-15 minutes, repeating as needed to achieve the best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea, also known as nettle tea, is a great remedy for dry skin. The plant contains many nutrients and antioxidants that help to soothe and calm dry skin. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and irritation, allowing the skin to feel smoother and more comfortable. Nettle tea is also rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and iron, which are essential for healthy skin. When consumed as a tea, these nutrients are absorbed into the body, where they can help to lock in moisture and support the skin's natural barrier function. This can lead to softer, more supple skin that's less prone to dryness and irritation.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry skin because it has strong antioxidant properties that help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.
Tannins help dry skin by reducing inflammation and protecting it from damage caused by free radicals, which makes skin feel smoother and look healthier.
Polyphenols are good for dry skin because they have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage, reduce inflammation, and improve its natural barrier function to retain moisture.
Isorhapontigenin helps to soothe dry skin by reducing inflammation, calming irritation, and locking in moisture to keep skin hydrated and supple.

remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal teas for dry skin
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal teas that help with dry skin, start by boiling one teaspoon of loose-leaf tea or one tea bag in a cup of water for 3-5 minutes. Let it steep, then strain the liquid into another cup to remove the leaves. You can reuse the leaves for a second brew, but discard them after the second steep. Next, let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature. You can add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and a drizzle of honey to taste, as the acidity and sweetness can help to balance the bitterness of the tea. Camellia sinensis is a natural source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and moisturize dry skin. You can drink it hot or cold, and consider drinking it once or twice a day to see the benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tea, also known as lemon balm, is good for dry skin because it has soothing and moisturizing properties. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This can lead to dry, irritated, and rough skin. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling in the skin. Additionally, lemon balm tea is known to improve skin elasticity, which can help to make dry skin feel softer and more supple. It can also help to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, giving the skin a more youthful appearance. The tea can be used as a topical treatment by applying it directly to the skin or as a drink to improve overall skin health from the inside out.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for dry skin because it has strong antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage, lock in moisture, and improve skin elasticity.
Apigenin is good for dry skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm dry, itchy skin while also promoting the growth of new skin cells.
Luteolin is good for dry skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing dryness and promoting healthy skin hydration.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal teas that help with dry skin, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the tea plant, which is also known as green tea. Rinse the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities, and then chop them into smaller pieces to release their beneficial properties. Next, heat one cup of water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a temperature of around 200°F (93°C). Pour the hot water over the chopped tea leaves and let it steep for 1-3 minutes, depending on your desired level of strength and flavor. Strain the tea leaves from the liquid and discard them. To enhance the skin benefits, you can add a slice of fresh ginger or a few sprigs of dried calendula flowers to the tea while it steeps. Finally, enjoy the Camellia sinensis herbal tea hot or iced, and drink it 2-3 times a week to help soothe and hydrate dry skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea is a great way to help dry skin. It works by soaking deep into the skin to lock in moisture and reduce irritation. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that calm and soothe dry, itchy skin. When we drink aloe vera tea, it helps to hydrate our skin from the inside out. The antioxidants in aloe vera also help to protect our skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can make dry skin worse. Drinking aloe vera tea can also help to improve our skin's natural barrier function, making it more resistant to dryness and irritation. Aloe vera tea is often used to help people with conditions like eczema and psoriasis, which cause dry, itchy skin.

Flavonoids are good for dry skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and soothe irritated skin, promoting healthy moisture levels and improving skin elasticity.

Echinacea purpurea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling in the skin. As a result, our skin becomes softer, smoother, and more hydrated. remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is good for dry skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce redness and swelling in dry skin. The tea also has antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Licorice root is rich in glycyrrhizin, a substance that helps lock in moisture and soothe dry skin. Drinking the tea may help to improve skin hydration and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help to calm and soothe skin irritations, such as eczema and acne.

First, boil some water in a kettle or on the stove, then pour it over a teaspoon of the leaves in a cup or teapot. Let it steep for 3-5 minutes, depending on how strong you like your tea. You can also add other herbs like chamomile or calendula to enhance the benefits. Once the tea has steeped, strain it into another cup to remove the leaves, and add a bit of honey or lemon to taste. After drinking the tea, you can also use the cooled tea as a face or body mask to help lock in moisture and soothe dry skin. Simply apply the cooled tea to your skin, let it sit for 10-15 minutes, then rinse with warm water and pat dry.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tea, also known as comfrey tea, is a natural remedy for dry skin. The tea is made from the leaves of the comfrey plant, which is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. These nutrients help to soothe and moisturize dry skin, reducing inflammation and irritation. The antioxidants in the tea also protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and other skin problems. Additionally, comfrey tea contains allantoin, a compound that helps to lock in moisture and promote healthy skin cell growth. Drinking comfrey tea can also help to improve skin elasticity, making it feel softer and more supple. The tea is easily absorbed by the body, allowing the nutrients to reach the skin quickly and effectively. This can lead to a reduction in dry skin symptoms, such as itchiness and flakiness, and an improvement in the overall appearance of the skin.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for dry skin because they have powerful moisturizing properties that lock in moisture and help repair the skin's natural barrier, making them a great natural remedy for soothing and hydrating dry, irritated skin.

Flavonoids are good for dry skin because they help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow, which can help to soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Polyphenols are good for dry skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and repair dry skin, reducing irritation and promoting healthy skin cell regeneration.

Begin by boiling fresh water and then reducing the heat to a simmer. Add one teaspoon of loose-leaf tea for every 8 ounces of water, and let it steep for 1-3 minutes, depending on your desired strength of tea. Strain the tea leaves and discard them. You can enjoy the tea hot or iced, with or without adding honey or lemon to taste. To enhance the skin benefits, try adding other herbs like calendula or chamomile to the tea, as they are known for their soothing and moisturizing properties. Drink 1-2 cups of Camellia sinensis herbal tea per day to help hydrate your skin from the inside out, reducing the appearance of dryness and promoting overall skin health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis tea is a good treatment for dry skin. It's made from the flowers of the soapwort plant, which gets its name from the soap-like substance it produces. This plant has been used for centuries to clean and soften skin. The tea is rich in saponins, which are natural soap-like compounds. When you drink the tea, the saponins are absorbed into your body and can help to moisturize dry skin from the inside out. The tea can also help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin. Additionally, the antioxidants in the tea can help to protect your skin from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, drinking Saponaria officinalis tea may help to soften and calm dry, itchy skin.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for dry skin because they have strong antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from damage, reduce inflammation, and improve skin hydration by increasing the production of natural moisturizers.
Quercetin is good for dry skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm dry, itchy skin by reducing swelling and redness.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal teas that help with dry skin, start by boiling fresh or dried Camellia sinensis leaves in a pot of water. The ideal ratio is to use one teaspoon of leaves for every 8 ounces of water. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let the leaves steep for 3-5 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to catch the leaves, then discard the leaves. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be careful not to add too much sugar as it may counteract the benefits of the tea. Camellia sinensis is also known as green tea, and it contains antioxidants that help to soothe and moisturize dry skin. Drinking one to two cups of this tea per day may help to reduce dryness and irritation, leaving your skin feeling softer and more hydrated.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis tea, also known as green tea, is good for dry skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce redness and swelling in the skin. The antioxidants in green tea, called catechins, can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Free radicals are like tiny molecules that can harm the skin's cells, leading to dryness and irritation. Green tea can also improve blood flow to the skin, which helps to keep it hydrated and healthy. The flavonoids in green tea can even help to repair damaged skin cells, promoting a smoother and softer skin texture. Drinking green tea can also help to reduce stress, which can cause skin problems like dryness and irritation.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry skin because it helps to lock in moisture, reduce inflammation, and soothe irritation, making it a natural and effective remedy to hydrate and calm dry, itchy skin.

Catechins are good for dry skin because they have antioxidant properties that help to protect and repair the skin, reduce inflammation, and improve skin hydration by increasing the production of ceramides and other lipids.

Theaflavins are good for dry skin because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect and soothe dry, irritated skin, reducing redness and promoting healthy skin hydration.
Tannins are good for dry skin because they help to lock in moisture, soothe irritation, and protect the skin from environmental stressors due to their astringent and antioxidant properties.

remedies.image_alt: Camellia sinensis herbal teas for dry skin
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal teas that help with dry skin, you'll need some dried green tea leaves, a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, and a pot of boiling water. First, boil some water in a pot and pour it over the dried green tea leaves in the tea infuser or directly into the heat-resistant cup. Let it steep for about 3-5 minutes to allow the tea leaves to release their goodness. Next, strain the tea leaves and discard them. Now, you can add some honey or lemon to taste, but be aware that adding honey or sugar may increase the calorie count. As Camellia sinensis is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, the tea is thought to help soothe and moisturize dry skin. To get the most benefits, drink the tea 2-3 times a week and also use it as a face mask by steeping the tea leaves in hot water, letting it cool, and applying it to your face.

supplementary_best: To remedy dry skin, a combination of Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, and Glycyrrhiza glabra is effective. Calendula officinalis is known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and calm dry skin. Aloe vera, with its high water content and moisturizing properties, deeply hydrates and nourishes the skin, reducing dryness and flakiness. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation, while also providing moisture and protection to the skin. This combination of herbal teas helps to moisturize, soothe, and calm dry skin, making it an effective remedy.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dry skin, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can dry out your skin further or cause irritation. One such herb is 'Ginkgo biloba'. Ginkgo biloba can cause skin irritation, especially when consumed as a tea, due to its astringent properties. This means it can dry out the skin, making dry skin worse. Another herb to avoid is 'Ephedra sinica', also known as 'Mahuang'. This herb can cause skin problems, including dryness and irritation, due to its stimulant properties. Additionally, 'Ephedra sinica' can also increase heart rate and blood pressure, which may be a concern if you have certain health conditions. 'Rauvolfia serpentina' is another herb to avoid, as it can cause skin irritation and dryness due to its alkaloid properties. Lastly, 'Cinchona officinalis' should be avoided, as it contains quinine, which can cause skin problems, including dryness and itching, when consumed in large amounts.
supplementary_other_remedies: Coconut oil is a natural remedy that helps with dry skin. It has moisturizing properties that lock in moisture, making it hard for dry air to take it away. Coconut oil can be applied directly to the skin, making it a simple and effective way to keep dry skin hydrated. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated skin.  Honey is another natural remedy that helps with dry skin. It has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help prevent infections that can make dry skin worse. Honey also has moisturizing properties that help lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and soft.  Oatmeal baths are a natural remedy for dry skin that has been used for centuries. Oatmeal has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated skin, and it also helps to lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated. When applied to the skin, oatmeal can also help to calm and soothe itchy skin.  Cocoa butter is a natural remedy that helps with dry skin. It has moisturizing properties that help lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and soft. Cocoa butter also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can make dry skin worse.  These natural remedies can be used alone or in combination with each other to help with dry skin. They are a good alternative to harsh chemicals found in many skin care products, and they can be effective at keeping dry skin hydrated and healthy.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with dry skin often experience other health issues. One common problem is eczema, a condition where the skin becomes itchy, red, and inflamed. This is because dry skin lacks moisture, which makes it more prone to irritation. Medicinal herbs like calendula can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and itching. Aloe vera is another herb that can provide relief, as it contains soothing compounds that can calm dry, itchy skin.  Another issue people with dry skin may face is cracked heels or hands. This can be painful and increase the risk of infection. Aloe vera and tea tree oil can help to soften and moisturize the skin, reducing the risk of cracking.  Dry skin can also lead to skin conditions like psoriasis, where the skin cells grow too quickly and form scaly patches. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.  In addition, people with dry skin may experience joint pain or arthritis, which can be linked to inflammation in the body. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation.  Dry skin can also be a sign of an underlying condition like hypothyroidism, where the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough hormones. A medicinal herb like ashwagandha can help support thyroid function and reduce symptoms of dry skin.
